title: pypodman: add options to handle ssh host keys
body: Some hosts are creating new host keys every boot

comments.body: One doc question, code LGTM, @jwhonce WDYT?
Other then the environment variable this PR LGTM.

Interestingly, ssh host keys has been an "important security feature" upstream (machine) since 2015.
Yet nothing has happened yet.

It is not so easy to get those host keys _back_ from the initial install, even if the ssh keys goes _in_ now.
Doesn't mean it won't happen.

Think I fixed it. The only downside is that now it needs a "known_hosts" file instead.

i.e. so that you can override the path of `~user/.ssh/known_hosts`, when needed...

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@jwhonce Please make sure you approve and merge.

Only remaining issue is that each new IP/port will add a new line in the `known_hosts` file.

But I suppose that is to be expected, don't really want to start to _edit_ the host / start of the line:
`[localhost]:46565 ecdsa-sha2-nistp256 AAAAE2VjZHNhLXNoYTItbmlzdHAyNTYAAAAIbmlzdHAyNTYAAABBBKj1zFDfV90GraOkuLe8tQBIjKGaW7akYqd+UYx25ewqMrhzz/CYwCYHDivWUk1KOHiAYT/EpMWGknyJ/MDKxmA=`

As long as the IP remains constant (and the SSH port doesn't change), it should be fine...
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2119) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Guess this has to move to the new https://github.com/containers/python-podman repo, once the move is finished. I stayed from using `--ignore-hosts` in `config` but `$PODMAN_IGNORE_HOSTS` in `env` is OK...

i.e. mostly because unknown command parameters is a hard error, but unused environment variables are fine. The only annoyance is that ssh will ask you to confirm the host keys. Guess that is survivable ?


``` console
The authenticity of host '[127.0.0.1]:39003 ([127.0.0.1]:39003)' can't be established.
ECDSA key fingerprint is SHA256:6H8SkZ+DjQmRGCdhj/77CjxQsbm/ZsJoJ80Yhi30V3k.
Are you sure you want to continue connecting (yes/no)? 
```
The host key does survive `restart`, so you should only get one such question per machine (VM).

The work for known_hosts isn't completed yet anyway, but that would be much better than "ignore".

@afbjorklund: PR needs rebase.

This PR Needs to move to podman-python.
